input.background: Children out of School in America is a report on delinquency of school-age children prepared by the Children's Defense Fund (CDF), a government-sponsored organization. The report includes three reported results based on surveys conducted in specific regions. The details of the reported results and the corresponding facts are provided.
input.question: Analyze Reported result 1: $25 \%$ of the 16- and 17-year-olds in the Portland, Maine, Bayside East Housing Project were out of school. Fact: Only eight children were surveyed; two were found to be out of school. Identify the potential distortions in this result.

output.answer: The reporting of $25 \%$ is misleading because it is based on a very small sample size of only eight children. Reporting percentages instead of the actual numbers (2 out of 8) can lead to misinterpretation of the data. The small sample size makes it unreliable to draw any meaningful inferences.

meta_info.caption: More Misleading Descriptive Statistics- Delinquent Children
meta_info.chapter: Methods for Describing Sets of Data
meta_info.section: 2.10 Distorting the Truth with Descriptive Techniques
